_doc.hook_md_contract: T1171: Each hook script ('.ps1') has a companion '.md' self-description file at the same path (e.g. '.cursor/hooks/g-hk-session-start.md' for '.cursor/hooks/g-hk-session-start.ps1'). The hook.md uses the 5-section template (# Hook / ## Fires On / ## What It Does / ## Side Effects / ## Related Tasks) defined by '@g-hook-create'. The runtime contract is: when a hook fires under 'preToolUse' (or any other event), the harness SHOULD read the matching hook.md and inject its content as 'additional_context' so the agent knows what the hook just did or why a tool call was blocked. See 'skills/g-skl-platform-cursor/SKILL.md' §3a and §11.
_doc.hook_md_authoring: Use '@g-hook-create <hook-name> <event>' to scaffold a new pair atomically. See 'commands/g-hook-create.md'.
_doc.hook_md_source_of_truth: Source the human-facing hook description from the 'hook.md' file; this 'hooks.json' is for wiring only. Do not duplicate the description here.
